title: Governments Tighten Rules on Social Media as Global Age Bans Spread
subtitle: Australia doubles fines for tech companies as more nations consider strict limits on youth access to online platforms.

summary.en: Australia has announced it will double the maximum penalty for social media companies that fail to keep children under 16 off their platforms. This move follows reports that many young users are still finding ways to access banned apps. Other countries, including the UK, are now planning similar laws to protect teenagers from online harm. Tech companies face growing pressure as public trust in their safety measures continues to fall. Experts and officials argue that current regulations are not enough to stop the risks of digital addiction.
summary.zh: 澳洲宣布將對未能將十六歲以下兒童阻絕於平台之外的社群媒體公司，將最高罰款金額提高一倍。此舉是在有報告指出許多年輕使用者仍能找到方法存取遭禁用的應用程式後所做出的。包括英國在內的其他國家，目前也正計畫制定類似法律，以保護青少年免受網路傷害。隨著公眾對科技公司安全措施的信任度持續下降，這些公司正面臨日益增長的壓力。專家與官員主張，目前的監管措施不足以遏止數位成癮帶來的風險。

paragraphs.en: The Australian government has announced a major increase in the penalties for social media companies that fail to keep children under 16 off their platforms. Under the new rules, the maximum fine for breaking the law will double to $99 million. This decision comes after officials found that many children are still using banned apps despite a law that started in December 2025. The Australian government stated that these harsher penalties are necessary because tech companies are not doing enough to follow the law.
paragraphs.zh: 澳洲政府宣布大幅提高對社群媒體公司的罰款，以懲罰未能將十六歲以下兒童阻絕於平台之外的業者。根據新規定，違法行為的最高罰款將翻倍至九千九百萬美元。此項決策是在官員發現儘管二零二五年十二月已實施相關法律，仍有許多兒童在使用遭禁用的應用程式後所做出的。澳洲政府表示，這些更嚴厲的懲罰是有必要的，因為科技公司在遵守法律方面做得還不夠。

paragraphs.en: According to the BBC, the Australian eSafety Commissioner will now have more power to demand evidence from social media companies about how they are following the rules. Investigations are currently looking into five major platforms: Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok, and YouTube. Although the ban was introduced with high expectations, enforcement has proven difficult. A report from the eSafety Commission found that seven out of 10 children who had an account before the ban still have some access to social media. Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said he was heartened by the global interest in the law, but added that it is clear big tech companies are still not doing enough to protect children.
paragraphs.zh: 根據英國廣播公司（BBC）報導，澳洲電子安全專員（eSafety Commissioner）現在將擁有更大權力，要求社群媒體公司提供關於其如何遵守規定的證據。目前調查正針對五大平台進行：臉書（Facebook）、Instagram、Snapchat、TikTok 以及 YouTube。儘管這項禁令在推出時備受期待，但執行上卻困難重重。電子安全委員會的一份報告發現，在禁令實施前就擁有帳號的兒童中，仍有七成的人仍能以某種方式使用社群媒體。澳洲總理艾班尼斯（Anthony Albanese）表示，他對全球對此法律的關注感到欣慰，但也補充說，顯然大型科技公司在保護兒童方面仍做得不足。

paragraphs.en: Australia is not the only country taking action. The global trend of setting a minimum age for social media is growing. In June 2026, the UK announced plans to introduce a similar ban for children under 16 by the spring of 2027. Other nations, including Indonesia, Malaysia, Austria, France, and Norway, are also looking at different ways to restrict access for young people. In Germany, recent statistics show that many teenagers spend several hours a day on their phones, leading to calls for stronger controls. Experts in Germany have suggested that platforms should be held more accountable for their content and that there should be safer default settings for minors.
paragraphs.zh: 澳洲並非唯一採取行動的國家。全球對於設定社群媒體最低使用年齡的趨勢正日益增長。二零二六年六月，英國宣布計畫在二零二七年春季前，針對十六歲以下兒童實施類似的禁令。包括印尼、馬來西亞、奧地利、法國和挪威在內的其他國家，也正在研擬各種限制年輕人使用網路的方法。在德國，近期統計數據顯示許多青少年每天花數小時在手機上，這引發了要求加強管控的呼聲。德國專家建議，平台應對其內容負起更多責任，並應為未成年人提供更安全的預設設定。

paragraphs.en: This shift in policy is partly driven by a decline in public trust. Arturo Béjar, a former employee at Meta, told The Guardian that parents around the world share a common fear about their children going online. He noted that many young people have already experienced harmful situations that could have been prevented. Recent court cases in the United States have also ruled that companies like Meta designed products that were intentionally addictive. While Meta has said it disagrees with these rulings and plans to appeal, the legal pressure is clearly influencing politicians in other parts of the world.
paragraphs.zh: 這種政策轉變部分源於公眾信任度的下降。曾任職於 Meta 的貝哈爾（Arturo Béjar）告訴《衛報》（The Guardian），世界各地的家長對於孩子上網都有共同的擔憂。他指出，許多年輕人已經經歷過本可預防的有害情況。美國近期的法院判決也裁定，像 Meta 這樣的公司設計的產品具有刻意讓人成癮的特性。雖然 Meta 表示不同意這些裁決並計畫上訴，但法律壓力顯然正在影響世界其他地區的政治人物。

paragraphs.en: However, the path to regulation is not simple. Some tech companies have expressed frustration, arguing that inconsistent rules make it difficult to design effective safety measures. A source from one tech company told The Guardian that rushed and heavy regulations might not actually lead to safer designs. They argued that when countries act without a clear, consistent plan, it can lead to high levels of circumvention, where users simply find ways to bypass the restrictions. Meanwhile, the tech industry continues to spend large amounts of money on lobbying—the act of trying to influence government decisions—to argue against strict new rules.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，監管之路並不容易。一些科技公司表達了挫折感，認為不一致的規定使得設計有效的安全措施變得困難。一家科技公司的消息人士告訴《衛報》，倉促且沉重的監管可能實際上無法帶來更安全的設計。他們主張，當各國在沒有明確、一致的計畫下採取行動時，可能會導致高比例的規避行為，即使用者會輕易找到繞過限制的方法。與此同時，科技產業持續投入大量資金進行遊說——即試圖影響政府決策的行為——以反對嚴格的新規定。

paragraphs.en: Despite these challenges, the debate seems to have changed. In the past, many believed that social media companies could manage their own safety issues. Today, there is a growing consensus among governments that they must step in. In addition to age bans, some countries are considering other measures, such as overnight curfews and limits on features like infinite scrolling, which keeps users on an app for as long as possible. The UK government is currently looking at these options as part of its upcoming legislation.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管面臨這些挑戰，辯論的焦點似乎已經改變。過去，許多人認為社群媒體公司可以自行管理其安全問題。如今，各國政府之間已達成越來越多的共識，認為必須介入干預。除了年齡限制外，一些國家正在考慮其他措施，例如夜間宵禁，以及限制如「無限捲動」等功能，因為這類功能會讓使用者盡可能長時間地停留在應用程式上。英國政府目前正將這些選項納入其即將推出的立法考量中。

paragraphs.en: For many families, these changes cannot come soon enough. As more governments follow Australia’s lead, the pressure on social media companies to prove they can provide a safe environment will only increase. Whether these new laws will be enough to change the digital habits of a generation remains to be seen. For now, the message from regulators is clear: the era of self-regulation for big tech is coming to an end, and the focus is shifting toward strict, legally enforced protection for young users.
paragraphs.zh: 對許多家庭而言，這些改變來得正是時候。隨著越來越多國家跟隨澳洲的腳步，社群媒體公司在證明其能提供安全環境方面所面臨的壓力只會越來越大。這些新法律是否足以改變這一代人的數位習慣，仍有待觀察。目前，監管機構傳達的訊息很明確：大型科技公司自我監管的時代即將結束，焦點正轉向針對年輕使用者、具法律強制力的嚴格保護。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become involved in a difficult situation or argument in order to help or improve it.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指政府介入管理社交媒體。文中：Today, there is a growing consensus among governments that they must step in.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Required to explain your actions or decisions and accept responsibility for them.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於商業或法律語境，指對行為負責。文中：Experts in Germany have suggested that platforms should be held more accountable for their content and that there should be safer default settings for minors.

keyTerms.definitionEn: An example or action that others follow.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（引導），這裡作名詞，指澳洲的先例。文中：As more governments follow Australia’s lead, the pressure on social media companies to prove they can provide a safe environment will only increase.

sources.name: BBC News
sources.title: Australia to double maximum penalty for platforms in breach of social media ban

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: ‘Tech firms are losing the public’: social media age bans near tipping point

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: Will Germany impose an age limit for social media?

previousArticles.title: Australia Increases Penalties as Global Social Media Bans Grow
previousArticles.summaryEn: Australia is doubling its penalties for social media companies that fail to keep children under 16 off their platforms. The government argues that tech giants are not doing enough to follow the law despite removing millions of accounts. Recent research suggests that most teenagers are still finding ways to access these sites. Meanwhile, other countries are following Australia's lead by introducing their own age-based restrictions. The global debate continues over how to protect children from the addictive nature of digital platforms.

previousArticles.title: Australia Increases Penalties for Social Media Companies
previousArticles.summaryEn: Australia is doubling the fines for social media companies that fail to keep children under 16 off their platforms. The government argues that tech giants are not doing enough to follow the law. Despite the removal of millions of accounts, research shows that most teenagers are still finding ways to access these sites. New rules will give officials more power to demand information from companies. Other countries are now looking at Australia's approach as they consider their own safety laws.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason the Australian government decided to increase the maximum fine for social media companies?

options.A: To generate more tax revenue from large technology corporations.
options.B: Because evidence showed that many children are still accessing banned apps.
options.C: To force companies to pay for the legal costs of recent court cases.
options.D: Because the eSafety Commissioner requested more funding for new investigations.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第一段，政府決定提高罰款是因為官員發現，儘管 2025 年 12 月實施了禁令，但許多兒童仍在使用被禁止的應用程式。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 C 提到的法院案件發生在美國，與澳洲政府提高罰款的直接動機無關；選項 D 雖然提到 eSafety Commissioner 有更多權力，但提高罰款的動機是為了應對禁令執行不力的現況，而非為了籌措調查經費。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the tech industry's stance on the new government regulations?

options.A: They believe that strict government laws are the most effective way to protect children.
options.B: They are actively working with governments to ensure all countries have identical safety rules.
options.C: They are concerned that a lack of global consistency will lead to users bypassing the rules.
options.D: They have fully accepted that the era of self-regulation is over and are redesigning their apps.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第五段，科技公司消息人士指出，各國法規不一致（inconsistent rules）會導致用戶更容易繞過限制（circumvention），這暗示了產業認為缺乏全球統一標準會削弱法規的有效性。選項 A 與文中提到的「科技公司對法規感到沮喪」矛盾；選項 B 錯誤，因為文中提到公司正花錢遊說反對嚴格的新規定；選項 D 過於武斷，文中並未表示科技公司已完全接受並放棄抵抗。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the phrase 'the era of self-regulation' refer to?

options.A: A period where companies were responsible for setting their own safety standards.
options.B: A time when users were required to regulate their own screen time.
options.C: A new policy where governments allow companies to write their own laws.
options.D: A system where parents were solely responsible for monitoring their children's online behavior.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。文中提到「the era of self-regulation for big tech is coming to an end」，結合第六段提到的「In the past, many believed that social media companies could manage their own safety issues」，可知這指的是過去科技公司被允許自行管理平台安全、無需政府強制介入的時期。選項 B、C、D 皆曲解了「self-regulation」在商業語境中指企業自主管理而非政府強制監管的含義。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article?

options.A: Social media platforms have successfully improved their safety features for minors since 2025.
options.B: The United States is leading the global effort to ban social media for children under 16.
options.C: Governments are shifting from relying on tech companies' voluntary safety efforts to implementing strict, legally enforced regulations.
options.D: Tech companies are successfully using lobbying to prevent governments from passing any new laws.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文核心在於探討全球政府（以澳洲為首）如何從過去信任科技公司自行管理，轉向透過嚴格法律手段來限制未成年人使用社群媒體。選項 A 與事實相反，文中強調執行困難且成效不彰；選項 B 錯誤，文中指出澳洲是領頭羊，且美國僅是提到法院案例；選項 D 雖然提到遊說，但並非文章主旨，文章最後明確指出政府介入已成趨勢，遊說並未阻止此潮流。
